cases.scenario: A trusted local desktop PTY pane is live. The boundary cue is hidden (local target), clipboard writes are admitted under preview, no shared role applies, and the recovery posture forbids auto-rerun even on the happy path.
A trusted remote PTY pane lost transport. The summary forces reconnect, names host-or-workspace drift, requires renewed intent, and keeps clipboard posture on the remote bridge under preview.

A participant holds a one-shot writer grant on a shared terminal. Live authority is narrowed, the row cites the typed control_grant_ref, recording is workspace-scoped, and clipboard posture is bound to the shared scope.
A view-only observer is bound to a shared terminal. The row is read-only-degraded, no shared write authority is implicit, clipboard posture is not applicable, and recording state is visible.
Admin policy blocks all clipboard writes on a remote pane. The summary cites blocked_by_policy with a typed denial reason and a class-only audit label.

A prior session restores as transcript evidence. Live authority is recovered_transcript_only, clipboard posture is not applicable, paste is forced no-auto-submit, and the recovery posture cites the fresh-session command id.
A prior session ended cleanly with no scrollback. Live authority is ended_requires_fresh_session and the row routes the user through the fresh-session command id; no transcript is implied.
Restore was declined by policy after a protocol-budget quarantine. Live authority is authority_blocked, renewed intent is required, and the fresh-session command id is wired in.
A support export carries only metadata: summary, boundary, restore class, timestamps. No transcript body crosses the boundary.
A support export carries a bounded scrollback snapshot under the support_bundle_scoped redaction class. Each line carries its own redaction class, digest, and observed_at.
An AI tool call admits a promoted slice. The packet binds the slice to a typed promoted-range provenance with admission ref and timestamps, and the summary class is rewritten to ai_promoted_slice.
